h2h_summary: Portugal and Uzbekistan have never met at senior international level; the World Cup 2026 clash on June 23 is the first-ever fixture between these two nations.
team_style_home: Portugal play a possession-based, attacking 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 under Roberto Martinez, emphasising width, technical quality in midfield, and quick transitions with pace on the flanks.
team_style_away: Uzbekistan under Fabio Cannavaro deploy a deep, compact defensive block in a 4-5-1 or 5-4-1 shape, prioritising defensive discipline and looking to break quickly through Shomurodov on the counter.
wc_history_home: Portugal have appeared in nine World Cups, reaching third place in 1966 (led by Eusebio) and fourth place in 2006, but have never lifted the trophy.
wc_history_away: Uzbekistan are making their maiden FIFA World Cup appearance in 2026, becoming the first Central Asian nation and third former Soviet republic to qualify for the tournament.
group_context: Group K features Portugal, Colombia, DR Congo and Uzbekistan, with Portugal as heavy favourites and Colombia the second-ranked side; for Uzbekistan, a debut World Cup point would be historic, while Portugal need a win here to build momentum ahead of a tougher final group game against Colombia.
strengths_home: World-class attacking depth with Ronaldo, Leao, Fernandes and Ramos providing multiple goal threats
Experienced and composed defensive unit anchored by Ruben Dias and Champions League-winning goalkeeper Diogo Costa
Champions League-winning core (Vitinha, Joao Neves, Nuno Mendes) bringing elite-level club form into the tournament
strengths_away: Organised defensive structure under Cannavaro designed to frustrate higher-ranked opponents
Khusanov's Manchester City pedigree provides genuine elite-level quality at the back
Shomurodov's European experience and goal threat (44 international goals) gives Uzbekistan a credible attacking outlet on the counter
